text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy little village nestled among rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver knew everything there was to know about history. He could talk about Ancient Egypt's pyramids longer than anyone.

One frosty evening, Oliver heard whispers of an odd cat. Every moonbeam seemed to dance around it. The cat would meow strangely. Children wouldn't believe it.

That's my story. Why should kittens believe? Anyway, one night, Oliver decided he'd had enough. He swooped down with his feathers.

"Alright," Oliver said softly.

The cat just blinked.

"If you tell me sweet things I might say how lovely your fur looks."

The cat meowed softly. Oliver told it stories from far-off lands. It listened.

That afternoon, Oliver rested. But then he heard little fluttery sounds. He opened his eyes. There were small creatures looking up.

"These treasures," Oliver thought. He gathered berries and nuts.

Night came.

"
